fields.label: Hostname
fields.name: hostname
fields.help: the Hostname is the eindeutige device name in the CLI-Prompt. Er erleichtert documentation, Fehlersuche, Syslog-Auswertung and verhindert Verwechslungen for mehreren Routern/Switches.

fields.label: Domain
fields.name: domain
fields.help: the Domain-name is under Cisco IOS for the RSA key generation requires, when SSH enabled are should. Beispiel: hoeferhome.local or firma.local.

fields.label: Enable Secret
fields.name: enableSecret
fields.help: The enable secret protects privileged EXEC mode. It should always be used instead of enable password because it is stored more securely.

fields.label: password min.
fields.name: minPass
fields.help: Sets a minimum length for new passwords. Useful for hardening. Typical values are 10, 12, or higher.

fields.label: no domain lookup
fields.name: noDomainLookup
fields.help: Prevents DNS lookups for mistyped commands. Without this command, the router attempts to resolve typing errors as host names, which causes delays.

fields.label: password encryption
fields.name: servicePass
fields.help: Encrypts simple passwords in the configuration using type 7. This is not strong protection, but it prevents plain-text display of basic passwords.

fields.label: Time zone
fields.name: clockTimezoneName
fields.help: Kurzname the Standardzeitzone for the command clock timezone, z. B. CET. the value is only the Bezeichnung; the UTC-offset is separately angegeben.

fields.label: UTC offset hours
fields.name: clockTimezoneHours
fields.help: Stundenabweichung the Standardzeit relative to UTC. for Deutschland is the Standardwert 1. Negative values are possible, z. B. -5.

fields.label: UTC offset minutes
fields.name: clockTimezoneMinutes
fields.help: additional Minutenabweichung relative to UTC. Meist 0; for Zeitzonen with halben hours beispielsweise 30.

fields.label: Daylight saving time name
fields.name: clockSummerName
fields.help: Kurzname the daylight saving time for clock summer-time, z. B. CEST. empty lassen, when no daylight saving time konfiguriert are should.

fields.label: Daylight saving time rule
fields.name: clockSummerRule
fields.help: Complete rule section after the daylight-saving-time name. Example: recurring last Sun Mar 2:00 last Sun Oct 3:00. A date-based IOS rule can be entered instead.

fields.label: Firma
fields.name: company
fields.help: Variable {COMPANY}. is only in the Banner ersetzt, when du sie dort verwendest.

fields.label: Location
fields.name: location
fields.help: Variable {LOCATION}. Useful when multiple sites or labs are involved.

fields.label: Admin/Kontakt
fields.name: admin
fields.help: Variable {ADMIN}. here can a Mailadresse, Rufnummer or Betriebsstelle shows.

fields.label: MOTD Banner
fields.name: motd
fields.help: MOTD appears before or in the Login. Nutze ihn for rechtliche notices, warnings and Kontaktinformationen. Variablen: {HOSTNAME}, {DATE}, {TIME}, {DEVICE_TYPE}, {LOCATION}, {ADMIN}, {COMPANY}.

fields.label: Login Banner
fields.name: loginBanner
fields.help: The login banner is displayed when accessing the console or VTY lines. Useful for clear warnings and ownership information.

fields.label: RSA Bits
fields.name: rsaModulus
fields.help: RSA key length for SSH. 2048 bits is the standard value. Packet Tracer commonly accepts 1024 or 2048. Use 2048 bits or higher on real devices.

fields.label: SSH Timeout Sek.
fields.name: sshTimeout
fields.help: time in Sekunden, wie lange a SSH-authentication offen remain darf.

fields.label: SSH Retries
fields.name: sshRetries
fields.help: maximum number Authentifizierungsversuche per SSH-connection.

fields.label: SSH Source If
fields.name: sshSourceIf
fields.help: Optionales source interface for SSH, z. B. Vlan99 or Loopback0.

fields.label: SSH Logging Events
fields.name: sshLogEvents
fields.help: Enables SSH-Event-Logging, if from the IOS supported.

fields.label: SSH Algorithm
fields.name: sshAlgorithm
fields.help: Optional. Configure this only if the target IOS version supports the command.

fields.label: VTY start
fields.name: vtyStart
fields.help: Startnummer the VTY-Line. Meist 0.

fields.label: VTY end
fields.name: vtyEnd
fields.help: Last VTY line number. Traditional ranges use 0 4; many devices support 0 15.

fields.label: VTY Login
fields.name: vtyLoginMode
fields.help: login local nutzt local username-Accounts. login authentication default nutzt AAA. password login nutzt a Line-password.

fields.label: VTY password
fields.name: vtyPassword
fields.help: only for password login relevant. Sonst empty lassen.

fields.label: VTY ACL
fields.name: vtyAclSsh
fields.help: optional: access-class for SSH-source networks, z. B. 10 or MGMT-SSH.

fields.label: Exec Timeout
fields.name: vtyExecTimeout
fields.help: Automatic logout in minutes and seconds, for example 10 0.

fields.label: Logging Sync
fields.name: vtyLoggingSync
fields.help: logging synchronous verhindert, that Konsolenmeldungen the Eingabe disrupt.

fields.label: Privilege
fields.name: vtyPrivilege
fields.help: Optionales privilege level on the VTY-Line. Meist empty lassen and privileges via user/AAA steuern.

fields.label: AAA local
fields.name: aaaLocal
fields.help: Enables aaa new-model with local authentication when no RADIUS or TACACS+ module is active. When RADIUS or TACACS+ is configured, the default method is generated centrally there to avoid conflicting aaa authentication login default commands.

fields.label: Console Timeout
fields.name: execTimeout
fields.help: Automatic console logout in minutes and seconds. Example: 10 0 disconnects the session after 10 minutes of inactivity.

fields.label: Console Logging Sync
fields.name: consoleLoggingSync
fields.help: logging synchronous verhindert, that Konsolenmeldungen the Eingabe disrupt.

fields.label: Console Login
fields.name: consoleLogin
fields.help: Optional. login local on the console requires at least one local user. VTY and SSH settings are configured exclusively in the SSH module.

fields.label: RADIUS: name|IP|Auth-Port|Acct-Port|Key|Timeout|Retransmit
fields.name: radiusList
fields.help: multiple RADIUS-Server possible. Auth-Port meist 1812, Accounting 1813. Timeout/Retransmit optional. Beispiel: RAD1|192.168.99.10|1812|1813|Secret|5|3

fields.label: TACACS+: name|IP|Key
fields.name: tacacsList
fields.help: multiple TACACS+-Server possible. TACACS+ trennt authentication, Autorisierung and Accounting especially gut for Adminzugriffe.

fields.label: AAA Radius Gruppe
fields.name: aaaRadiusGroup
fields.help: Generates aaa group server radius and bindet the RADIUS-Server in These Gruppe a.

fields.label: Use AAA RADIUS
fields.name: aaaUseRadius
fields.help: when Ja, is AAA Login/Authorization/Accounting gegen RADIUS with local Fallback generated.

fields.label: Source Interface
fields.name: radiusSourceIf
fields.help: optional: source interface for RADIUS-Pakete, z. B. Loopback0 or Vlan99. Hilft for Firewalls and stabiler Serverkommunikation.

fields.label: NTP: server|prefer
fields.name: ntpList
fields.help: NTP synchronisiert the Uhrzeit. important for Syslog, Zertifikate, Fehlersuche and Ereigniskorrelation. Format: Server-IP|prefer optional.

fields.label: Syslog: server|level
fields.name: syslogList
fields.help: Syslog sendet Logs on a zentralen Server. Level: emergencies, alerts, critical, errors, warnings, notifications, informational, debugging.

fields.label: SNMPv3: group|user|auth|authpass|priv|privpass
fields.name: snmpList
fields.help: SNMPv3 is significantly more secure for monitoring than SNMPv2 because it supports authentication and encryption. Example: MON|snmpuser|sha|authpass|aes|privpass.

fields.label: Timestamps
fields.name: logTimestamps
fields.help: Enables detailed Zeitstempel for Logs and Debugs.

fields.label: Buffered size
fields.name: logBuffered
fields.help: size the local Logpuffers.

fields.label: Trap Level
fields.name: logTrap
fields.help: Syslog Trap-Level.

fields.label: Archive Log Config
fields.name: archiveLog
fields.help: Logs configuration changes.

fields.label: Login Audit
fields.name: loginAudit
fields.help: Logs erfolgreiche and fehlgeschlagene Logins.

fields.label: Archive Path
fields.name: archivePath
fields.help: Optionaler Speicherort for Konfigurationsarchive, z. B. flash:/archive-$h or scp://user:pass@server/path. Nutzt same archive-configuration mode wie Config-Logging, deshalb is it here combined.

fields.label: Archive write-memory
fields.name: archiveWriteMemory
fields.help: Archives the configuration for write memory, if from the IOS supported.

fields.label: Archive time-period
fields.name: archiveTimePeriod
fields.help: Optionales Archivintervall in minutes. empty lassen, when only manually stored are should.

fields.label: user|Privilege|Secret
fields.name: privUserList
fields.help: Central local-user management. This list replaces the former separate user module so username commands are not generated twice. Privilege level 15 grants full access; lower levels are restricted.

fields.label: ParserView|Secret|Command
fields.name: parserViewList
fields.help: Optional parser views for restricted CLI permissions. Availability depends on IOS and is useful when users must not receive full administrative privileges.

fields.label: Interface|description|routed/access/trunk|IP/VLAN/Allowed|mask
fields.name: interfacesList
fields.help: routed = Layer-3-Port with IP-address. access = end-device port in exactly a VLAN. trunk = connection between Switches or to Router/Firewall with mehreren VLANs.

fields.label: Range|description|access/trunk|VLANs|PortFast|BPDU Guard|PortSecurity
fields.name: rangesList
fields.help: Configures viele ports at the same time. PortFast only for end-device ports. BPDU Guard schaltet ports ab, when dort unerwartet a Switch/BPDU auftaucht. PortSecurity begrenzt MAC-addresses.

fields.label: Interface|Sub-ID|VLAN-ID|IP|mask
fields.name: subifList
fields.help: Router-on-a-Stick: a physisches Interface verarbeitet multiple VLANs via 802.1Q Tags. Sub-ID entspricht meist the VLAN-ID, must but technisch not identical be.

fields.label: Parent Interface
fields.name: rosParentIf
fields.help: Physisches Router-Interface, the as trunk to the Switch used is. on the Switch must the Port as trunk konfiguriert be.

fields.label: Native VLAN optional
fields.name: rosNativeVlan
fields.help: Optionales Native VLAN. only eintragen, when du intentionally a Native VLAN without Tagging use willst. Meist empty lassen or unbenutztes VLAN use.

fields.label: Parent no shutdown
fields.name: rosParentNoShutdown
fields.help: Sets no shutdown on the physischen Parent-Interface. the IP-addresses are located on the Subinterfaces.

fields.label: VLANs/Subinterfaces: VLAN-ID|name|gateway-IP|mask
fields.name: rosVlanList
fields.help: a Zeile per VLAN/Subinterface. Only Router-Subinterfaces generated: interface Parent.VLAN, encapsulation dot1Q, ip address, no shutdown. VLANs selbst must on the Switch existieren.

fields.label: Trust Interfaces
fields.name: qosTrustInterfaces
fields.help: Enables DSCP Trust / Auto-QoS Trust on Interfaces. platform-dependent.

fields.label: Class|DSCP|Policy|PriorityPercent
fields.name: qosPolicyList
fields.help: Generates simple class-map/policy-map Struktur.

fields.label: Interface|profiles|description|Wert1|Wert2
fields.name: interfaceProfileList
fields.value:
Gi0/1|access|Client Port|10|
Gi0/2|voice|Telefon + Client|10|30
Gi0/48|trunk|Uplink|10,20,30,99|999
Gi0/10|unused|Nicht genutzt|999|
fields.help: profiles: access, voice, trunk, uplink, server, ap, unused, routed. Wert1/Wert2 per after profiles VLANs, Native VLAN or IP/mask.

fields.label: Interface|Allowed VLANs|Native VLAN|description|Guard(root/loop)
fields.name: trunkUplinkList
fields.help: Generates saubere trunk/Uplink-configuration with nonegotiate and optional Root/Loop Guard.

fields.label: provider VLAN name Prefix
fields.name: qinqVlanPrefix
fields.help:
Name prefix for automatically generated provider or service VLANs. Example: QINQ_SVLAN_200.

Command components:
vlan <Provider-VLAN> = creates the outer service VLAN.
name <Prefix><Provider-VLAN> = assigns a unique name to the S-VLAN.

fields.label: System MTU optional
fields.name: qinqSystemMtu
fields.help:
Optional and platform-dependent. QinQ adds 4 bytes of overhead for every additional 802.1Q tag, so a value of 9000 is not universally required.

Practical guidance:
Leave empty = do not generate an MTU command.
1522/1526 = often sufficient for normal Ethernet plus VLAN/QinQ overhead, depending on the platform.
9216 = jumbo/provider-core scenario, only when supported end to end.
9000 is a host jumbo-frame value and is no longer used as the default.

Command when explicitly configured:
system mtu jumbo <Bytes>

Note: Many Catalyst platforms require a reload afterwards.

fields.label: globally dot1q tunneling
fields.name: qinqGlobalDot1q
fields.help:
Optionaler notice-Schalter. classic Catalyst-configuration nutzt meistens per Port switchport mode dot1q-tunnel. Global dot1q-/Ethertype-options are stark IOS-/IOS-XE-/platform-dependent and are deshalb not arbitrarily as command generated.

Relevante Syntax per after platform validate, z. B. dot1q tunneling ethertype <0x8100|0x88A8>.

fields.label: Interface|provider VLAN|description|L2ProtocolTunnel
fields.name: qinqTunnelList
fields.value:
Gi0/10|200|Kunde A QinQ|cdp,stp,vtp
Gi0/11|201|Kunde B QinQ|stp
fields.help:
Port-based QinQ for customer-facing switchports. The port is assigned to a provider/service VLAN and operates in dot1q-tunnel mode.

Command components:
interface <Interface> = customer-facing port at the provider or switch edge.
description <Description> = port documentation.
switchport mode dot1q-tunnel = enables QinQ tunnel mode.
switchport access vlan <Provider-VLAN> = outer S-VLAN/provider VLAN.
l2protocol-tunnel <cdp|stp|vtp> = tunnels selected Layer 2 protocols when supported by the platform and IOS.

Interface MTU is no longer generated by default. MTU is managed globally through the optional system MTU because mtu <Bytes> is frequently platform-dependent or invalid on Layer 2 switchports.

Format: Interface|Provider-VLAN|Description|L2ProtocolTunnel.
L2ProtocolTunnel may contain cdp, stp, vtp, or all.

fields.label: only L3-Switch: Parent|SubID|provider VLAN|Customer-VLAN|IP optional|mask optional|description
fields.name: qinqSubifList
fields.help:
Double-Tagged Layer-3-Subinterfaces. These Zeilen are only in the device type L3-Switch generated. in the L2-Switch are sie intentionally skipped. Router is for the QinQ-module not mehr angeboten.

Command parts:
interface <Parent>.<SubID> = Subinterface on routed Parent-Interface.
encapsulation dot1Q <provider VLAN> second-dot1q <Customer-VLAN> = outer S-VLAN and inner C-VLAN.
ip address <IP> <mask> = optional, when the Subinterface Layer-3-Abschluss is.

Format: Parent|SubID|provider VLAN|Customer-VLAN|IP optional|mask optional|description.

fields.label: Load-Balance
fields.name: etherLoadBalance
fields.help: optional: port-channel load-balance, depending of platform.

fields.label: PortChannel|Interfaces|Mode|access/trunk|VLANs|description|Native VLAN
fields.name: etherExtList
fields.help: LACP active/passive, PAgP desirable/auto or on. generated Interface-Range and Port-Channel.

fields.label: Interface|role|description|Access-VLAN|Voice-VLAN|Allowed VLANs|Native VLAN|PortChannel|IP|mask|VRF
fields.name: interfaceRoleList
fields.value:
Gi0/1|access|Client|10||||||
Gi0/2|voice|Telefon|10|30|||||
Gi0/48|uplink|Uplink Core|||10,20,30,99|999||||
Gi1/0/1|pc-member|Bundle Member|||||1|||
Gi0/10|unused|Nicht genutzt|999||||||
fields.help:
Purpose: Zentrales Rollenmodell for ports.

Command parts:
access generated switchport mode access, access VLAN, spanning-tree portfast and bpduguard.
voice generated additionally switchport voice VLAN.
trunk generated mode trunk, allowed VLANs, native VLAN and nonegotiate.
uplink adds root guard.
ap generated portfast trunk.
pc-member generated channel-group <ID> mode active.
routed generated no switchport, optional ip vrf forwarding and ip address.
unused setzt shutdown.

Format: Interface|role|description|Access-VLAN|Voice-VLAN|Allowed VLANs|Native VLAN|PortChannel|IP|mask|VRF.

fields.label: VLANs: VLAN-ID|name
fields.name: vlanList
fields.help: VLANs separate Layer 2 broadcast domains. VLAN 1 should not be used as a production or management VLAN.

fields.label: SVIs: VLAN-ID|IP|mask
fields.name: sviList
fields.help: SVI = Switch Virtual Interface. on L2-Switch for Management-IP. on L3-Switch as Default-gateway for Clients in the VLAN.

fields.label: Default gateway L2
fields.name: defaultGateway
fields.help: only for Layer-2-Switches relevant. so that the Switch be Management-network verlassen can, z. B. for SSH from anderen Netzen.

fields.label: Data VLAN
fields.name: voipDataVlan
fields.help:
data-VLAN for Clients behind a IP-Telefon. is as normales VLAN created and on phone ports as switchport access VLAN set.

Command parts:
switchport access VLAN <VLAN-ID> = VLAN for PC-/Datenverkehr.

fields.label: Data VLAN name
fields.name: voipDataVlanName
fields.help: name the data-VLANs. Dient the Lesbarkeit in show VLAN brief and in the documentation.

fields.label: Voice VLAN
fields.name: voipVoiceVlan
fields.help:
Voice-VLAN for IP phones. is as VLAN created and on phone ports with switchport voice VLAN set.

command:
switchport voice VLAN <VLAN-ID>

fields.label: Voice VLAN name
fields.name: voipVoiceVlanName
fields.help: name the Voice-VLANs. recommendation: kurz and eindeutig, z. B. VOICE, VOIP or TELEFONIE.

fields.label: CDP enable
fields.name: voipCdp
fields.help:
CDP hilft Cisco-IP-Telefonen, the Voice-VLAN automatically to erkennen.

command:
cdp run

fields.label: LLDP enable
fields.name: voipLldp
fields.help:
LLDP is vendor-neutral. Many phones use LLDP-MED to discover the voice VLAN.

Commands:
lldp run
lldp med-tlv-select network-policy

fields.label: QoS Modus
fields.name: voipQosMode
fields.help:
QoS-Behandlung for phone ports.

auto qos voip cisco-phone = Cisco-AutoQoS for phone ports.
mls qos trust dscp = Trusts DSCP markings.
mls qos trust cos = Trusts CoS markings on 802.1Q Frames.

fields.label: DHCP Pool name
fields.name: voipDhcpPool
fields.help: Name of the DHCP pool for phones. The pool is generated only when network, mask, and gateway are provided.

fields.label: Voice network
fields.name: voipNetwork
fields.help:
network address the Voice-VLANs for the DHCP pool.

command:
network <network> <mask>

fields.label: Voice mask
fields.name: voipMask
fields.help: subnet mask the Voice-Netzes for the DHCP pool.

fields.label: Voice gateway
fields.name: voipGateway
fields.help:
Default gateway for Telefone. in the rule the SVI-/Router-address the Voice-VLANs.

command:
default-router <gateway-IP>

fields.label: DNS Server optional
fields.name: voipDns
fields.help: Optionaler DNS-Server for Telefone. empty lassen, when no DNS per DHCP verteilt are should.

fields.label: TFTP/CUCM optional
fields.name: voipTftp
fields.help:
Optional TFTP or CallManager server for phones. Cisco phones commonly use DHCP option 150.

Command:
option 150 ip <TFTP-IP>

fields.label: Excluded optional
fields.name: voipExcluded
fields.help: Optionaler DHCP-Ausschlussbereich. Format: start-IP End-IP or only a einzelne IP. empty lassen, when nichts ausgeschlossen are should.

fields.label: Interface|description|Access-VLAN|Voice-VLAN|PortFast|BPDU Guard|QoS|PortSecurity
fields.name: voipInterfaceList
fields.value:
Gi0/2|Phone + client|10|30|Yes|Yes|auto qos voip cisco-phone|No
Gi0/3|Office phone|10|30|Yes|Yes|mls qos trust dscp|Yes
fields.help:
One line per phone port.

Command components:
interface <Interface> = target port.
description <Description> = port documentation.
switchport mode access = access port for phone and PC.
switchport access vlan <Access-VLAN> = data VLAN.
switchport voice vlan <Voice-VLAN> = voice VLAN.
spanning-tree portfast = accelerates port activation for endpoint devices.
spanning-tree bpduguard enable = protects against switches connected to the endpoint port.
auto qos voip cisco-phone or mls qos trust ... = QoS trust or AutoQoS.
PortSecurity Yes = sticky MAC plus restrict.

Format: Interface|Description|Access-VLAN|Voice-VLAN|PortFast|BPDU Guard|QoS|PortSecurity.

Device filter: This module is available only for Layer 2 and Layer 3 switches because switchport voice vlan is not a router-port command.

fields.label: DHCP Snooping VLANs
fields.name: dhcpSnoopVlans
fields.help: DHCP snooping protects against unauthorized DHCP servers. Enter the VLANs in which clients use DHCP, for example 10,20,99.

fields.label: DAI VLANs
fields.name: daiVlans
fields.help: Dynamic ARP Inspection protects against ARP spoofing. It requires a reliable DHCP-snooping binding table or static bindings.

fields.label: Range|description|Access-VLAN|Voice-VLAN|PortFast|BPDU Guard|PortSecurity|Storm-Level|Shutdown|Nonegotiate
fields.name: accessBaseList
fields.help: Generates common Access-Port-configuration PortFast, BPDU Guard, Port-Security, Storm-Control and Shutdown for ungenutzte ports.

fields.label: Interval in seconds
fields.name: errdisableInterval
fields.help: time bis to the automatischen Wiederaktivierung.

fields.label: Causes, a per Zeile
fields.name: errdisableCauseList
fields.help: common Ursachen: bpduguard, psecure-violation, udld, link-flap, storm-control.

fields.label: Spanning-Tree-Modus
fields.name: stpGlobalMode
fields.help: Selects the active Spanning Tree protocol. The interface automatically displays only the options that apply to the selected mode.

fields.label: Plattformprofil
fields.name: stpPlatformProfile
fields.help: Controls compatibility warnings. The tool generates Cisco IOS/IOS-XE syntax; Packet Tracer may support advanced commands only partially.

fields.label: Switch-Modell optional
fields.name: stpSwitchModel
fields.help: Optional model name for documentation and compatibility checks, for example C9200L-24P-4G.

fields.label: IOS-/IOS-XE-Version optional
fields.name: stpIosVersion
fields.help: Optional version information, for example 17.12.4. It is used for guidance but does not independently change the target platform.

fields.label: Strict compatibility
fields.name: stpStrictCompatibility
fields.help: for Ja are protokollfremde options not exportiert and as warning gemeldet.

fields.label: Extend system ID
fields.name: stpExtendSystemId
fields.help: Generates spanning-tree extend system-id.

fields.label: Path cost method
fields.name: stpPathcostMethod
fields.help: Selects the kurze or lange STP-Pfadkostenskala.

fields.label: PortFast default
fields.name: stpPortfastDefault
fields.help: Enables PortFast globally for geeignete Access-ports.

fields.label: BPDU Guard default
fields.name: stpBpduguardDefault
fields.help: Enables BPDU Guard globally for PortFast-ports.

fields.label: BPDU Filter default
fields.name: stpBpdufilterDefault
fields.help: Enables BPDU Filter globally. Use it only deliberately because it can suppress BPDUs.

fields.label: Loop Guard default
fields.name: stpLoopguardDefault
fields.help: Enables Loop Guard globally.

fields.label: EtherChannel Guard
fields.name: stpEtherchannelGuard
fields.help: Detects incorrect configured EtherChannels.

fields.label: UplinkFast (Legacy PVST)
fields.name: stpUplinkFast
fields.help: Legacy-PVST-Konvergenzoptimierung. not for Rapid-PVST or MST export.

fields.label: BackboneFast (Legacy PVST)
fields.name: stpBackboneFast
fields.help: Legacy-PVST-Optimierung for indirekte Linkfehler. not for Rapid-PVST or MST export.

fields.label: VLAN/Range for assistant
fields.name: stpVlanTarget
fields.help: a VLAN or section, z. B. 10 or 10,20,30-40.

fields.label: Root role
fields.name: stpVlanRootRole
fields.help: Schnellassistent for Root Primary, Root Secondary or a manual bridge priority.

fields.label: Bridge priority
fields.name: stpVlanPriorityValue
fields.help: Only valid Cisco values in increments of 4096 can be selected.

fields.label: multiple root rolen: VLAN|primary/secondary
fields.name: stpRootList
fields.help: Beispiel: 10|primary. per Zeile is a root role generated.

fields.label: Multiple VLAN priorities: VLAN|Priority
fields.name: stpVlanPriorityList
fields.help: Beispiel: 20|8192. allowed are 0 bis 61440 in Schritten of 4096.

fields.label: VLAN-Timer: VLAN|Hello|Forward|MaxAge
fields.name: stpVlanTimersList
fields.help: Leere Spalten are ausgelassen. Beispiel: 10|2|15|20.

fields.label: VLAN-Interfaceparameter: Interface|VLANs|PortPriority|Cost
fields.name: stpVlanInterfaceList
fields.help: Beispiel: Gi1/0/1|10|64|10000. Port-Priority: 0 bis 240 in Schritten of 16.

fields.label: interface profiles: Interface|profiles|VLANs|Native VLAN
fields.name: stpInterfaceProfileList
fields.help: Profiles: Client, Server, Access Point, Switch Uplink, Port Channel, and Provider Uplink. VLAN columns are used for documentation and plausibility checks.

fields.label: Interface|PortFast|BPDU Guard|BPDU Filter|Guard|LinkType|PortPriority|Cost
fields.name: stpInterfaceList
fields.help: PortFast: Ja, trunk or Nein. Guard: root, loop or empty. LinkType is only for Rapid-PVST/MST exportiert.

fields.label: Root Guard Interfaces
fields.name: stpRootGuardIfs
fields.help: One interface per line. Root Guard prevents a superior root bridge from being elected through this port.

fields.label: Loop Guard Interfaces
fields.name: stpLoopGuardIfs
fields.help: One interface per line. Loop Guard protects against unidirectional Spanning Tree failures.

fields.label: MST Regionsname
fields.name: stpMstName
fields.help: must on allen Switches derselben MST-Region exakt identical be.

fields.label: MST Revision
fields.name: stpMstRevision
fields.help: Revisionsnummer 0 bis 65535. Sie must within a Region identical be.

fields.label: MST Max hops
fields.name: stpMstMaxHops
fields.help: Generates spanning-tree mst max-hops. The usual valid range is 1 through 40.

fields.label: MST-instance for assistant
fields.name: stpMstTargetInstance
fields.help: instance 1 bis 4094. instance 0 is the is/CIST and is not for a eigenes VLAN-mapping used.

fields.label: MST root role
fields.name: stpMstRootRole
fields.help: Schnellassistent for root role or manual instance-priority.

fields.label: MST bridge priority
fields.name: stpMstPriorityValue
fields.help: Only valid values in increments of 4096.

fields.label: MST: instance|VLANs|Priority|Root
fields.name: stpMstInstanceList
fields.help: Beispiel: 1|10,20|4096| or 2|30-40||primary.

fields.label: MST-Interfaceparameter: Interface|instance|PortPriority|Cost
fields.name: stpMstInterfaceList
fields.help: Beispiel: Port-channel1|1|64|20000.

fields.label: MST region comparison: Switch|name|Revision|instance:VLANs;...|Digest optional
fields.name: stpMstPeerRegionList
fields.help: Compares other Switches with the local Region. Beispiel: SW2|FIRMA|1|1:10,20;2:30-40|ABCD.

fields.label: BPDU-Guard Errdisable Recovery
fields.name: stpErrdisableBpduguard
fields.help: Generates errdisable recovery cause bpduguard.

fields.label: Errdisable interval in seconds
fields.name: stpErrdisableInterval
fields.help: is together with BPDU-Guard Recovery ausgegeben. Typischer value: 300 Sekunden.

fields.label: Reset: scope|target|parameter
fields.name: stpResetList
fields.help: Scopes: globally, VLAN, mst, interface. Beispiele: VLAN|10|priority or interface|Gi1/0/1|portfast.

fields.label: Live-STP-preview
fields.name: stpLivePreview
fields.help: Displays only the currently generated STP configuration section. Hidden fields are not exported when they do not apply to the selected mode.

name: switchSecExt
title: DHCP Snooping / DAI / IP Source Guard

fields.label: DHCP Snooping VLANs
fields.name: sseDhcpVlans
fields.help: VLANs for DHCP Snooping.

fields.label: DAI VLANs
fields.name: sseDaiVlans
fields.help: VLANs for Dynamic ARP Inspection.

fields.label: Trusted Interfaces
fields.name: sseTrustedIfs
fields.help: Uplinks/DHCP-Server-ports, denen DHCP/ARP Trusts is.

fields.label: RateLimit: Interface|Rate
fields.name: sseRateLimitIfs
fields.help: DHCP Snooping Rate Limit per Interface.

fields.label: IP Source Guard Interfaces
fields.name: sseSourceGuardIfs
fields.help: Enables ip verify source per Interface.

fields.label: Interface|Maximum|Sticky ja/nein|Violation|AgingTime|AgingType
fields.name: portSecExtList
fields.help: Erweiterte Port-Security per Interface.

fields.label: VLAN|name|IPv4-network|mask|gateway|IPv6-gateway|VRF|DHCP ja/nein|DNS|Helper|HSRP-VIP|OSPF-Area|BGP ja/nein
fields.name: vlanIpPlanList
fields.help:
Purpose: VLAN-/IP-Adressplan centrally pflegen.

commands:
VLAN <ID>, name <name>, interface VLAN <ID>, ip vrf forwarding <VRF>, ip address <gateway> <mask>, ipv6 address, ip helper-address, standby, ip dhcp pool.

Command parts:
VRF is before ip address set.
DHCP ja generated a DHCP pool.
Helper generated ip helper-address.
HSRP-VIP generated standby <VLAN> ip <VIP>.
OSPF/BGP are as Kommentarhinweis exportiert.

fields.label: IPv4-Routing
fields.name: ipRouting
fields.help: Enables with ip routing the IPv4-Routing on a Layer-3-Switch. on Routern is Routing meist bereits active; the command is trotzdem korrekt importiert and for selection generated.

fields.label: target|mask|Next-Hop/Interface|AD optional
fields.name: staticRouteList
fields.help: Static routes define fixed paths. The administrative distance is optional; a higher value can create a floating static route that is used only when a dynamic or lower-distance route fails.

fields.label: Process-ID
fields.name: ospfPid
fields.help: OSPF Process-ID is only locally on this Router relevant. Sie must not on Nachbarroutern equal be. Sie identifiziert the local OSPF-instance.

fields.label: configuration method
fields.name: ospfConfigMode
fields.help: process-based generated network ... area ... in the OSPF-process. interface-based generated ip ospf <process> area <Area> directly under the Interfaces. mixed allowed beide Varianten at the same time.

fields.label: Router-ID
fields.name: ospfRid
fields.help: Unique 32-bit identifier in IPv4 format, for example 1.1.1.1. It identifies the OSPF router in LSAs, adjacencies, and the link-state database and must be unique within the OSPF domain.

fields.label: Ref-Bandwidth
fields.name: ospfRef
fields.help: Referenzbandbreite in Mbit/s for the Cost-Berechnung. 10000 = 10 Gbit/s. on allen OSPF-Routern equal set.

fields.label: Adjacency-Logging
fields.name: ospfLogAdjacency
fields.help: Ja generated log-adjacency-changes. Detail generated log-adjacency-changes detail and protokolliert additional state changes the OSPF-adjacencies.

fields.label: Networks: network|Wildcard|Area
fields.name: ospfNetList
fields.help: Enables OSPF on Interfaces, deren IP in the section passt. Area 0 is Backbone. Interfaces on demselben Link must dieselbe Area haben.

fields.label: Interfaces: Interface|Area
fields.name: ospfInterfaceAreaList
fields.help: Interfacebasierte OSPF-activation. per Zeile: Interface|Area. Beispiel: GigabitEthernet1/0/48|0 generated under the Interface ip ospf <Process-ID> area 0.

fields.label: Passive default
fields.name: ospfPassiveDefault
fields.help: passive-interface default prevents unintended OSPF adjacencies. Add no passive-interface only for the interfaces that should form neighbors.

fields.label: Default originate
fields.name: ospfDefaultOriginate
fields.help: Yes generates default-information originate. Always generates default-information originate always and advertises the default route even when no local 0.0.0.0/0 route exists.

fields.label: No-passive Interfaces
fields.name: ospfNoPassiveList
fields.help: Interfaces, on denen OSPF neighbors bilden darf.

fields.label: Stub Areas
fields.name: ospfStubAreas
fields.help: Generates area X stub no-summary.

fields.label: Interface|Cost|Priority|NetworkType
fields.name: ospfIfSettings
fields.help: Optionale Interface-parameter for OSPF.

fields.label: IS-IS Tag
fields.name: isisTag
fields.help: Local process name. Interfaces are assigned to this IS-IS process later. The tag does not need to be identical across the network.

fields.label: NET
fields.name: isisNet
fields.help: Network Entity Title. Identifiziert the IS-IS-Router. must eindeutig be. Beispiel: 49.0001.0000.0000.0001.00.

fields.label: Interfaces: Interface
fields.name: isisIfList
fields.help: IS-IS is interfacebezogen enabled. Trage a Interface-Bezeichnung per Zeile a, z. B. GigabitEthernet0/0.

fields.label: BGP AS
fields.name: bgpAs
fields.help: Autonomous-System-number the local Routers. Gleiches AS in the neighbors = iBGP, anderes AS = eBGP.

fields.label: Router-ID
fields.name: bgpRid
fields.help: Unique BGP router ID. It should remain stable; a loopback address is commonly used. It is important for BGP identification and troubleshooting.

fields.label: neighbors: Neighbor-IP|Remote-AS|description optional
fields.name: bgpNeighborList
fields.help: BGP baut no neighbors automatically. each Neighbor must explizit with IP and Remote-AS konfiguriert are.

fields.label: Networks: network|mask
fields.name: bgpNetworkList
fields.help: BGP announced only networks, the matching in the routing table existieren. Format: network|mask.

fields.label: Erweiterte Neighbor-options: Neighbor|update source|Multihop|Next-Hop-Self|Password|Max-Prefix|Soft-in|Default-Originate
fields.name: bgpExtNeighborList
fields.help: Optionale Erweiterungen per Neighbor. must to the Neighbor from the oberen Liste passen.

fields.label: HSRP: Interface|Gruppe|VIP|Priority|preempt
fields.name: hsrpList
fields.help: HSRP provides a virtual gateway IP address. Clients use the VIP as their default gateway. The higher priority wins, and preempt allows the router to resume the active role after recovery.

fields.label: VRF: name|RD|description
fields.name: vrfList
fields.help: Generates VRF definitions.

fields.label: Interface|VRF|IP|mask
fields.name: vrfInterfaceList
fields.help: Assigns interfaces to a VRF. Caution: ip vrf forwarding removes an existing IP address on real devices.

fields.label: VRF|target|mask|Next-Hop
fields.name: vrfRouteList
fields.help: static VRF-routes.

fields.label: PrefixList: name|Seq|permit/deny|Prefix
fields.name: prefixListAdv
fields.help: Prefix-Listen for Routingfilter.

fields.label: RouteMap: name|permit/deny|Seq|PrefixList|Set-command
fields.name: routeMapList
fields.help: Route-Map with optionalem match prefix-list and set-command.

fields.label: Distribute: Routingprozess|ACL/Prefix|in/out
fields.name: distributeList
fields.help: simple distribute-list-Zuweisung.

fields.label: SLA-ID|target|Source-Interface|Frequency|Track-ID
fields.name: ipSlaList
fields.help: Generates ICMP IP SLA with Track.

fields.label: target|mask|PrimaryNH|TrackID|BackupNH
fields.name: trackedRouteList
fields.help: Generates getrackte Primary Route plus Floating Backup Route.

fields.label: Area-Typ: Area|stub/totally-stubby/nssa/totally-nssa
fields.name: ospfAreaTypeList
fields.help:
command: area <Area> stub, area <Area> stub no-summary, area <Area> nssa or area <Area> nssa no-summary.

notice: Area-Typen must on allen Routern the Area zusammenpassen.

fields.label: Auth: Interface|md5/clear|Key
fields.name: ospfAuthList
fields.help:
commands for md5: ip ospf authentication message-digest and ip ospf message-digest-key 1 md5 <Key>.

Befehlsteile: Interface = OSPF-Interface, md5/clear = Authentifizierungsart, Key = gemeinsamer key.

fields.label: Timer: Interface|Hello|Dead
fields.name: ospfTimerList
fields.help:
commands: ip ospf hello-interval <Sekunden> and ip ospf dead-interval <Sekunden>.

notice: neighbors must gleiche Timer use.

fields.label: Summary: Area|network|mask
fields.name: ospfSummaryList
fields.help:
command: area <Area> range <network> <mask>.

Einsatz: summary on ABR between OSPF-Areas.

fields.label: Neighbor Policy: Neighbor|RouteMap-in|RouteMap-Out|Prefix-in|Prefix-Out|Timer keepalive hold
fields.name: bgpPolicyNeighborList
fields.help:
commands: neighbor <IP> route-map <name> in/out, neighbor <IP> prefix-list <name> in/out, neighbor <IP> timers <keepalive> <hold>.

Route-Maps and Prefix-Lists must separately available be.

fields.label: IPv6 AF: Neighbor|Remote-AS|Prefix|RouteMap-in|RouteMap-Out
fields.name: bgpAfIpv6List
fields.help:
commands: address-family ipv6 unicast, neighbor <IPv6> remote-as <AS>, neighbor <IPv6> activate, network <Prefix>, optional route-map in/out.

Einsatz: BGP IPv6 separately of IPv4 enable.

fields.label: ip cef
fields.name: mplsCef
fields.help:
Enables Cisco Express Forwarding. MPLS requires CEF on classic IOS-/IOS-XE-Plattformen as foundation for Label Switching.

command:
ip cef

fields.label: MPLS globally enable
fields.name: mplsGlobal
fields.help:
Enables MPLS globally. additionally must MPLS on the Transit-/Core-Interfaces with mpls ip enabled are.

command:
mpls ip

fields.label: Label Protocol
fields.name: mplsLabelProtocol
fields.help:
Sets the Label-Distribution-protocol defined. in modernen IOS-/IOS-XE-Designs is LDP the common default.

command:
mpls label protocol ldp

fields.label: LDP Router-ID Interface
fields.name: mplsLdpRouterId
fields.help:
Sets the stabile LDP Router-ID. Empfohlen is a loopback interface, the via the IGP erreichbar is.

command:
mpls ldp router-id <Interface> [force]

fields.label: Router-ID force
fields.name: mplsLdpRouterIdForce
fields.help:
Erzwingt the selected LDP Router-ID. Nutze force intentionally, da bestehende LDP-Sessions kurz again aufgebaut are can.

Befehlsteil:
force

fields.label: MPLS Interfaces: Interface|description optional|MPLS-MTU optional|LDP-IGP-Sync Ja/Nein
fields.name: mplsInterfaceList
fields.help:
Enables MPLS on transit and core interfaces. An MPLS MTU may optionally be set per interface. Leaving it empty is usually preferable when the platform already handles the overhead correctly or MTU is planned separately.

Command components:
interface <Interface> = core or transit interface.
description <Description> = optional documentation.
mpls ip = enables MPLS/LDP on the interface.
mpls mtu <Bytes> = optional and platform-dependent; do not set 9000 indiscriminately.
mpls ldp igp sync = optional; prevents blackholing while LDP and the IGP are not synchronized.

Format: Interface|Optional description|Optional MPLS MTU|LDP-IGP Sync Yes/No

fields.label: LDP Neighbor password: Neighbor-IP|password
fields.name: mplsLdpPasswordList
fields.help:
Configures optional LDP neighbor passwords. Use this only when the peer is configured identically.

Command:
mpls ldp neighbor <Neighbor-IP> password <Password>

fields.label: MP-BGP AS optional
fields.name: mplsBgpAs
fields.help:
local BGP-AS for MPLS L3VPN/MP-BGP. when empty, is no VPNv4-BGP generated.

command:
router bgp <AS>

fields.label: VPNv4 update source optional
fields.name: mplsVpnv4UpdateSource
fields.help:
update source for VPNv4-Peers. Typisch is Loopback0, so that the Session regardless from the physischen Link remains.

command:
neighbor <Peer-IP> update source <Interface>

fields.label: VPNv4 Peers: Peer-IP|Remote-AS|description optional|Send-Community extended Ja/Nein
fields.name: mplsVpnv4Peers
fields.help:
Generates MP-BGP VPNv4-Peers for MPLS L3VPN. Send-Community extended is for Route-Targets praktisch required.

Command parts:
neighbor <Peer-IP> remote-as <AS>
address-family vpnv4
 neighbor <Peer-IP> activate
 neighbor <Peer-IP> send-community extended

Format: Peer-IP|Remote-AS|description optional|Send-Community extended Ja/Nein

fields.label: L3VPN VRFs: VRF|RD|RT Import|RT Export|description optional
fields.name: mplsVpnVrfList
fields.help:
Creates VRF definitions for MPLS Layer 3 VPNs with an RD and route targets. If the VRF module is already in use, duplicate VRF definitions may be generated; the duplicate check reports them.

Command components:
vrf definition <VRF>
 rd <RD>
 route-target import <RT>
 route-target export <RT>
 address-family ipv4

Format: VRF|RD|Import RT|Export RT|Optional description

fields.label: VRF: name|RD|description
fields.name: vrfDefList
fields.help:
Format per line: Name|RD|Description.
Name = freely selectable VRF name that must be used exactly the same way for interfaces, static routes, and routing protocols.
RD = route distinguisher, for example 65000:10. It makes routes technically unique, especially when identical networks exist in multiple VRFs.
Description = optional text for documentation.
Export example:
ip vrf SERVER
 rd 65000:10
 description Server VRF

fields.label: SVI in VRF: VLAN|VRF|IPv4|mask|empty|name|IPv6/Prefix|IPv6 enable
fields.name: vrfSviList
fields.help:
Format per Zeile: VLAN|VRF|IPv4|mask|empty|name|IPv6/Prefix|IPv6 enable.
VLAN = VLAN-ID, z. B. 10.
VRF = name from VRF definitions.
IPv4/mask = gateway-address the VLANs within this VRF.
empty = Reservefeld, empty lassen, so that alte Vorlagen kompatibel remain.
name = VLAN-name.
IPv6/Prefix = optionales IPv6-gateway, z. B. 2001:db8:10::1/64.
IPv6 enable = ja, when additionally ipv6 enable set are should.
Exportbeispiel:
interface VLAN 10
 ip vrf forwarding SERVER
 ip address 192.168.10.1 255.255.255.0

fields.label: IPv4: VRF|target|mask|Next-Hop
fields.name: vrfStaticList
fields.help:
Command: ip route vrf <VRF> <Destination-Network> <Mask> <Next-Hop>.
VRF = routing table in which the route is installed.
Destination = target network, for example 0.0.0.0 for a default route.
Mask = subnet mask of the destination network.
Next hop = next router within the same VRF.

fields.label: IPv6: VRF|target/Prefix|Next-Hop
fields.name: vrfIpv6StaticList
fields.help:
Command: ipv6 route vrf <VRF> <IPv6-Prefix> <Next-Hop>.
VRF = IPv6 routing table.
Destination/prefix = for example ::/0 for a default route or 2001:db8:20::/64.
Next hop = IPv6 address of the next router. A link-local next hop usually also requires an outgoing interface.

fields.label: OSPFv2: VRF|Process|network|Wildcard|Area|Router-ID
fields.name: vrfOspfList
fields.help:
Command components:
VRF = routing table in which OSPF runs.
Process = local OSPF process ID; it does not need to match neighboring routers.
Network = IPv4 network activated by the network command.
Wildcard = inverse mask, for example 0.0.0.255 for /24.
Area = OSPF area, for example 0 for the backbone.
Router ID = optional but recommended; it must be unique within the OSPF domain.
Export example:
router ospf 10 vrf SERVER
 router-id 10.10.10.10
 network 192.168.10.0 0.0.0.255 area 0

fields.label: process: VRF|Process|empty|Router-ID
fields.name: vrfOspfv3List
fields.help:
Command parts:
VRF = routing table for IPv6-OSPF.
Process = OSPFv3-process ID.
empty = Reservefeld for compatibility, empty lassen.
Router-ID = OSPFv3 requires weiterhin a ID in the IPv4-Format.
Exportbeispiel:
router ospfv3 10
 address-family ipv6 vrf SERVER
  router-id 10.10.10.10

fields.label: Interface: Interface|VRF|Process|Area
fields.name: vrfOspfv3IfList
fields.help:
Command: interface <Interface> plus ospfv3 <Process> ipv6 area <Area>.
Interface = SVI or physical interface assigned to a VRF.
VRF = documentation and validation field; the actual VRF is inherited from the interface.
Process = must match the OSPFv3 process configured above.
Area = OSPFv3 area, usually 0 for the backbone.

fields.label: IPv4: VRF|AS|Neighbor|Remote-AS|network|mask
fields.name: vrfBgpIpv4List
fields.help:
Command parts:
VRF = BGP Address-Family, z. B. address-family ipv4 vrf SERVER.
AS = local BGP-AS.
Neighbor = IPv4-address the BGP-neighbors in this VRF.
Remote-AS = AS the neighbors. equal = iBGP, verschieden = eBGP.
network/mask = Prefix, the advertised is. Er must normalerweise in the routing table available be.

fields.label: IPv6: VRF|AS|Neighbor|Remote-AS|Prefix
fields.name: vrfBgpIpv6List
fields.help:
Command parts:
VRF = IPv6 Address-Family the VRF.
AS = local BGP-AS.
Neighbor = IPv6-address the neighbors.
Remote-AS = AS the neighbors.
Prefix = IPv6-network, the advertised is, z. B. 2001:db8:10::/64.
Exportbeispiel:
router bgp 65000
 address-family ipv6 vrf SERVER
  neighbor 2001:db8:ffff::2 remote-as 65010
  neighbor 2001:db8:ffff::2 activate
  network 2001:db8:10::/64

fields.label: IPv6 Interfaces: Interface|IPv6/Prefix|LinkLocal optional
fields.name: ipv6IfList
fields.help: IPv6-address with prefix, z. B. 2001:db8:10::1/64. Link-Local can manually set are, especially relevant for static routes via fe80::.

fields.label: Basis-Pools: Pool|network|mask|gateway|DNS
fields.name: dhcpPoolList
fields.help: Basis-DHCP pool. for Domain, Lease or Option 150 dieen Pools unten use.

fields.label: Excluded: start-IP|End-IP optional
fields.name: dhcpExcludedList
fields.help: addresses or Bereiche, the DHCP not vergeben darf.

fields.label: Erweiterte Pools: Pool|network|mask|gateway|DNS|Domain|Lease|Option150
fields.name: dhcpExtPoolList
fields.help: Erweiterter DHCP Pool with Domain, Lease and Option 150 for VoIP/TFTP.

fields.label: ACL: name|permit/deny|protocol|source|target
fields.name: aclList
fields.help: Extended ACL. source and target in the IOS-Format. Beispiel target 'any eq 22' allowed SSH. ACLs filter Traffic after direction and Interface.

fields.label: Zuweisung: Interface|ACL|in/out
fields.name: aclApplyList
fields.help: Weist ACL a Interface to. in checks incoming Traffic on the Interface, out checks ausgehenden Traffic.

fields.label: default ACL: number/name|permit/deny|source
fields.name: stdAclList
fields.help: default ACL filtert only after source. Gut for VTY/Management ACLs.

fields.label: Extended ACL assistant: name|permit/deny|protocol|source|target
fields.name: extAclList
fields.help: Extended ACL filtert after source, target and protocol/Port.

fields.label: assistant-Zuweisung: Interface|ACL|in/out
fields.name: aclAssignExtList
fields.help: Weist assistant-ACLs Interfaces to.

fields.label: IPv6 Static Routes: target/Prefix|Next-Hop/Interface
fields.name: ipv6StaticRoutes
fields.help: static IPv6-routes, auch via Link-Local + Interface.

fields.label: IPv6 ACL: name|permit/deny|protocol|source|target
fields.name: ipv6AclList
fields.help: IPv6 ACL rules.

fields.label: IPv6 ACL Apply: Interface|ACL|in/out
fields.name: ipv6AclApplyList
fields.help: Weist IPv6 ACL a Interface to.

fields.label: RA Suppress Interfaces
fields.name: ipv6RaSuppressIfs
fields.help: Suppresses router advertisements on Interfaces.

fields.label: DHCPv6 Relay: Interface|Destination
fields.name: dhcpv6RelayList
fields.help: DHCPv6 Relay target per Interface.

fields.label: OSPFv3 process
fields.name: ospfv3Process
fields.help: OSPFv3 process ID.

fields.label: Router-ID
fields.name: ospfv3Rid
fields.help: Router-ID for OSPFv3.

fields.label: Interface|Area
fields.name: ospfv3IfList
fields.help: Enables OSPFv3 IPv6 on Interfaces.

fields.label: BGP IPv6: AS|Neighbor|Remote-AS|Prefix|empty|update source|Route-Map-Out
fields.name: ipv6BgpList
fields.help: BGP for IPv6 without VRF. generated address-family ipv6 unicast, enabled Neighbor and advertised IPv6-prefixes.

fields.label: EIGRP IPv6: AS|Router-ID|Interface
fields.name: ipv6EigrpList
fields.help: EIGRP for IPv6. IOS/IOS-XE- and platform-dependent. requires ipv6 unicast-routing and Interface-activation.

fields.label: IS-IS IPv6: Tag|Interface
fields.name: ipv6IsisList
fields.help: IS-IS for IPv6. Uses address-family ipv6 and enables IS-IS on the interface. Useful in larger provider-style or core-style designs.

fields.label: ACL name
fields.name: aclAssistName
fields.help: command: ip access-list extended <name>. name the ACL, z. B. MGMT-SSH.

fields.label: Action
fields.name: aclAssistAction
fields.help: permit allowed relevant Traffic. deny blockiert relevant Traffic.

fields.label: Protocol
fields.name: aclAssistProtocol
fields.help: Protocol of the ACL rule. Port numbers are meaningful only for TCP or UDP.

fields.label: source Typ
fields.name: aclAssistSrcType
fields.help: any = each source. host = einzelne IP. network = network with Wildcard, z. B. 192.168.10.0 0.0.0.255.

fields.label: source value
fields.name: aclAssistSrc
fields.help: for host only IP eintragen. for network network + Wildcard eintragen. for any empty lassen.

fields.label: target Typ
fields.name: aclAssistDstType
fields.help: any = jedes target. host = einzelne target-IP. network = destination network with Wildcard.

fields.label: target value
fields.name: aclAssistDst
fields.help: for host only IP eintragen. for network network + Wildcard eintragen. for any empty lassen.

fields.label: Port
fields.name: aclAssistPort
fields.help: Port is as eq <Port> on the target adds. only for TCP or UDP.

fields.label: Interface Apply
fields.name: aclAssistInterface
fields.help: optional. when set, is the ACL with ip access-group on this Interface applied.

fields.label: Direction
fields.name: aclAssistDirection
fields.help: in = traffic enters the interface. out = traffic leaves the interface.

fields.label: no service pad
fields.name: hardNoPad
fields.help: Disables PAD Service.

fields.label: no ip source-route
fields.name: hardNoSourceRoute
fields.help: Disables IP Source Routing.

fields.label: no ip bootp server
fields.name: hardNoBootp
fields.help: Disables BOOTP Server.

fields.label: CDP active
fields.name: hardCdp
fields.help: Nein generated no cdp run.

fields.label: LLDP active
fields.name: hardLldp
fields.help: Ja generated lldp run.

fields.label: Unused Range
fields.name: hardUnusedRange
fields.help: optional: Interface-Range, the as ungenutzt shutdown set is, z. B. Gi0/10-24.

fields.label: NAT Typ
fields.name: natMode
fields.help: Selects which NAT type is exported. This prevents multiple NAT variants from being generated accidentally. Use All only when multiple NAT methods are intentionally combined.

fields.label: NAT Interfaces: Interface|inside/outside
fields.name: natIfList
fields.help: inside = internes network, outside = direction WAN/Internet. NAT requires korrekt markierte Interfaces.

fields.label: Overload: ACL|Outside-Interface
fields.name: natOverloadList
fields.help: PAT/NAT overload translates many internal source addresses to the outside interface address.

fields.label: Static NAT: InsideLocal|InsideGlobal
fields.name: natStaticList
fields.help: 1:1 NAT between interner and globaler address.

fields.label: Portforward: protocol|InsideLocal|InsidePort|InsideGlobal|GlobalPort
fields.name: natPortList
fields.help: Static PAT / Portweiterleitung.

fields.label: NAT Pool: name|StartIP|EndIP|mask|ACL
fields.name: natPoolList
fields.help: Dynamischer NAT Pool with ACL.

fields.label: NAT Exemption: RouteMap|ACL|Interface
fields.name: natExemptList
fields.help: foundation for NAT-Ausnahmen, z. B. VPN Traffic.

fields.label: GRE: TunnelID|IP|mask|Source|Destination

fields.help: GRE creates an unencrypted tunnel between two endpoints. Combine GRE with IPsec when encryption is required.

fields.label: One Cisco command per line
fields.name: customConfigList
fields.help: For commands that do not yet have a dedicated module. They are copied to the export exactly as entered. Caution: this field is not syntax-validated.
